summary: Tested Download URL/Exposé feature for EuroAdria Investment Intelligence Platform. All backend and frontend tests pass. The feature allows admins to add external PDF links (Google Drive, Dropbox) to articles, which display as a download button on the article page.

issues: Console shows many 'Failed to load resource: net::ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED' errors - likely due to empty REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in .env causing some API calls to fail. Core functionality works via proxy.

backend_create_article_with_downloadUrl.description: POST /api/admin/articles with downloadUrl field creates article with downloadUrl saved

backend_update_article_downloadUrl.description: PUT /api/admin/articles/{id} with downloadUrl field updates the downloadUrl

backend_get_article_returns_downloadUrl.description: GET /api/articles/{slug} returns downloadUrl field when it exists

backend_get_article_null_downloadUrl.description: GET /api/articles/{slug} returns null downloadUrl when not set

frontend_admin_download_section.description: Article editor form shows 'Download / Exposé' section with URL input field

frontend_admin_download_shows_value.description: downloadUrl input field shows current value when editing existing article

frontend_admin_download_persists.description: downloadUrl can be filled, saved, and persists after reload

frontend_article_download_button_appears.description: Download button ('Exposé herunterladen' with 'Download PDF' button) appears when article has downloadUrl

frontend_article_download_link_correct.description: Download button links to correct external URL with target=_blank

frontend_article_no_download_section.description: No download section appears when article has no downloadUrl

seed_data_creation: Test article with id=101 and slug='test-expose-download' already exists with downloadUrl set
context_for_next_testing_agent: Download URL/Exposé feature is fully working. Test file test_download_url.py covers all API endpoints. The frontend .env has empty REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL but the app works via proxy configured in package.json.
